checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
MapAnything is a universal feed-forward metric 3D reconstruction framework that infers camera parameters, point maps, and dense geometry from multi-view images in a single forward pass using a transformer architecture.

It directly supports Objective 3 (Unified Architecture) as the base geometric model, and Objective 1 (Data Scaling Strategy) by providing the multi-view reconstruction backbone to be trained on the large-scale geometric datasets (ScanNet++, RealEstate10K).

Aligns with the approved research topic of DataScience & Engineering.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
Aria Synthetic Environments (ASE) is a synthetic indoor-scene dataset from Meta's Project Aria, containing ~100,000 multi-room indoor scenes with simulated Aria-device trajectories (~2-minute sequences) populated with ~8,000 3D objects, providing 6DoF camera trajectories, 3D floor plans, 2D instance segmentation, and 2D range maps.

The dataset is used in this proposal for full fine-tuning of the unified geometry+semantics foundation model on top of the MapAnything pre-training. It is also one of the datasets MapAnything was originally pre-trained on, so it is required for continuing training in the proposal's data scaling strategy.

Aligns with the approved research topic, datascience and engineering.

checks.name: Prohibited Use Screening (LC 2.7)
checks.notes: No mention of sensitive terms in the documentation classified in Prohibited Use Screening in accordance with LC 2.7. The dataset is described for general computer vision, robotics, and AR/VR research with no reference to military, weapons, defense, surveillance, or intelligence applications.

checks.notes: Owner: Meta Platforms, Inc. (United States), Project Aria team. Non-D5 entity.

checks.notes: No user files provided.

checks.name: Sample Inspection
checks.notes:
No risk in LC 2.7 and LC 2.5 checks. Skipping inspection.

For reference, a visualization is available via the official tutorial notebook: projects/AriaSyntheticEnvironment/tutorial/ase_tutorial_notebook.ipynb

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
BlendedMVS is a large-scale synthetic multi-view stereo (MVS) dataset for training and evaluating 3D reconstruction and depth estimation models, providing multi-view RGB images of the same scene from different virtual camera viewpoints, ground-truth depth maps for each image, camera intrinsics/extrinsics, and scene geometry derived from rendered 3D models.

The dataset is used in this proposal as one of the geometric training datasets that scale the unified foundation model's reconstruction objective; it is also one of the datasets MapAnything was originally pre-trained on, so it is required for continuing training in the proposed full fine-tuning.

Aligns with the approved research topic, datascience and engineering.

checks.name: Prohibited Use Screening (LC 2.7)
checks.notes: No mention of sensitive terms in the documentation classified in Prohibited Use Screening in accordance with LC 2.7. The dataset is described as a multi-view stereo benchmark for 3D reconstruction with no reference to military, defense, weapons, surveillance, or intelligence applications.

checks.notes:
Primary creators: Yao Yao, Zixin Luo, Shiwei Li, Jingyang Zhang, Yufan Ren, Lei Zhou, Tian Fang, Long Quan. Yao Yao is now Associate Professor at Nanjing University (mainland China). The dataset originates from the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST).

Entity is from D5 country.

checks.notes: No user files provided.

checks.notes: BlendedMVS is a synthetic multi-view stereo dataset; contents are rendered scene images with depth maps and camera parameters. checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
DL3DV-10K is a large-scale real-world scene dataset (10,510 scenes) captured for deep-learning-based 3D vision and novel view synthesis research.

The dataset is used in this proposal as one of the geometric training datasets for the unified foundation model's reconstruction objective and is one of the datasets MapAnything was originally pre-trained on (cited in the proposal's Detailed Objective 1 alongside ScanNet++ and RealEstate10K).

Aligns with the approved research topic, datascience and engineering.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
ETH3D is a stereo matching, multi-view stereo, and SLAM / 3D reconstruction benchmark with ground-truth 3D geometry acquired with precise laser scanners.

The dataset is used in this proposal as one of the geometric reconstruction datasets that scale the unified foundation model's reconstruction objective; it is also one of the datasets MapAnything was originally pre-trained on, and the applicant relies on MapAnything's pre-processing scripts for it (download script: https://github.com/xeTaiz/map-anything/blob/main/data_processing/wai_processing/download_scripts/download_eth3d.py).

Aligns with the approved research topic, datascience and engineering.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
Dynamic Replica is a synthetic benchmark for dynamic stereo depth/disparity estimation, providing synchronized synthetic videos of people and animals in motion within scanned environments.

The dataset is used in this proposal as one of the geometric datasets that scale the unified foundation model's reconstruction objective on dynamic scenes; it is also one of the datasets MapAnything was originally pre-trained on, and is referenced via MapAnything's pre-processing scripts.

Aligns with the approved research topic, datascience and engineering.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
Mapillary Planet-Scale Depth is a metric depth estimation dataset containing approximately 750,000 images extracted from over 50,000 individual 3D reconstructions, captured by a broad range of camera types in diverse environments, weather conditions, seasons, times of day, and viewpoints, with real noise and motion patterns.

The dataset is used in this proposal as one of the metric-supervised depth datasets that scale the unified foundation model's reconstruction objective; it is also one of the datasets MapAnything was originally pre-trained on.

Aligns with the approved research topic, datascience and engineering.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
MegaDepth is a large depth estimation dataset built from Internet photo collections combined with Structure-from-Motion reconstruction and semantic labeling methods to generate large amounts of training data for single-view depth prediction.

The dataset is used in this proposal as one of the geometric training datasets that scale the unified foundation model's reconstruction objective; it is also one of the datasets MapAnything was originally pre-trained on.

Aligns with the approved research topic, datascience and engineering.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
MVS-Synth is a fully synthetic multi-view stereo dataset comprising 120 sequences (100 frames each) of urban scenes rendered from the video game Grand Theft Auto V, providing RGB images, ground-truth depth maps, and camera parameters per frame.

The dataset is used in this proposal as one of the synthetic multi-view datasets that scale the unified foundation model's reconstruction objective; it is also one of the datasets MapAnything was originally pre-trained on.

Aligns with the approved research topic, datascience and engineering.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
Parallel Domain 4D (ParallelDomain-4D) is a synthetic dynamic scene reconstruction dataset, used as a benchmark by the Generative Camera Dolly (GCD) work for extreme monocular dynamic novel view synthesis.

The dataset is used in this proposal as one of the synthetic dynamic-scene datasets that scale the unified foundation model's reconstruction objective; it is also one of the datasets MapAnything was originally pre-trained on.

Aligns with the approved research topic, datascience and engineering.

TartanAir is a photo-realistic simulation dataset for robot navigation, visual SLAM, and related perception tasks, providing multimodal ground-truth labels from LiDAR, IMU, customizable optical cameras (fisheye/pinhole/equirectangular), depth cameras, segmentation cameras, and event cameras.

The dataset (TartanAirV2 / TartanAirV2-WB) is used in this proposal as one of the synthetic scene-reconstruction datasets that scale the unified foundation model's reconstruction objective; it is also one of the datasets MapAnything was originally pre-trained on.

Aligns with the approved research topic, datascience and engineering.

Aligns with the approved research topic, datascience and engineering.

checks.notes: No mention of sensitive terms in the documentation classified in Prohibited Use Screening in accordance with LC 2.7. The dataset is described as a video panoptic segmentation benchmark.

checks.name: D5+M affiliation Screening (LC 2.5)
checks.notes:
Primary creators: Jiaxu Miao, Xiaohan Wang, Yu Wu, Wei Li, Xu Zhang, Yunchao Wei. Maintained at https://github.com/VIPSeg-Dataset/VIPSeg-Dataset.

The related paper's code is published in : https://github.com/VSPW-dataset/VSPW_code
The association of the lead authors is with Zhejiang University (CCAI) & Baidu Research China.  

Entities are from D5 country.

checks.name: Prompts / Fine-tuning Scripts
checks.notes: No user files provided.

checks.name: Sample Inspection
checks.notes:
No public preview is available. 
Sample inspection reveals that the object (visuals) are stack of JPG (i.e. video dataset) and the captions/labels of the categories do not match is the randomly selected samples. The content is was not sensitive. Samples available in /ibex/project/c2320/AIMCR/dataset-check/VIPSeg/VIPSeg_inspect.ipynb 

The PI confirms that they might exclude its labels from training in this case, but the raw video data will still be useful to us, they would like to proceed with this dataset as well. For due diligence, a question has been asked about the effect of this change on the alignment of this dataset with their research goals. It is understood that this dataset will now be used in the self-distillation component of your training scheme, where pseudo-labels are generated for data lacking ground truth.
Self-distillation: the model generates its own predictions for data where no real labels exist, then uses those predictions as training targets to improve itself iteratively, bootstrapping quality over time rather than relying on external ground truth. This implies that the lack of ground truth shall not affect the training scheme.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
MapAnything is an end-to-end transformer that regresses factored, metric 3D scene geometry from flexible inputs (images and optionally intrinsics, poses, depth), supporting feed-forward 3D reconstruction tasks (multi-image SfM, multi-view stereo, monocular metric depth estimation, registration, depth completion) in a single model.

In this proposal, MapAnything is the base pre-trained model on which the project will perform full fine-tuning to build the unified 3D-reconstruction + semantic-segmentation foundation model, as specified in the Model Readiness additional section: "Full fine tuning with additional datasets and training objectives". MapAnything is also cited in the proposal's State-of-the-Art section as one of the geometric foundation models motivating the unified approach (alongside VGGT and Depth Anything V3).

Aligns with the approved research topic, datascience and engineering.

checks.name: Prohibited Use Screening (LC 2.7)
checks.notes: No mention of sensitive terms in the documentation classified in Prohibited Use Screening in accordance with LC 2.7. The model is documented for general-purpose 3D reconstruction (SfM / MVS / depth / registration / depth completion).

checks.notes:
HuggingFace URL: https://huggingface.co/facebook/map-anything
GitHub URL: https://github.com/facebookresearch/mapanything

Owner: Meta AI / Meta Reality Labs (per the Model Readiness section)
Organization: Meta Platforms, Inc. (United States)

Non-D5 entity. No D5+M affiliation found.

checks.name: License / Permissions
checks.notes:
License: Apache License 2.0 (per the model card and the Model Readiness section).
Permissions:
- Use the model for any purpose, including commercial use
- Modify and create derivative works
- Distribute in source or binary form
Obligations:
- Include a copy of the license when redistributing
- Preserve required copyright/attribution notices (NOTICE where applicable)
- State changes when distributing modified versions
Grants: Express patent grant from contributors (with termination on patent litigation).

checks.name: Training Data Documentation
checks.notes:
Per the Model Readiness additional section, MapAnything was pre-trained on 189,552 3D reconstruction scenes drawn from 14 public datasets: BlendedMVS, Mapillary Planet-Scale Depth, ScanNet++ v2, Spring, TartanAirV2-WB, UnrealStereo4K, Aria Synthetic Environments, DL3DV-10K, Dynamic Replica, MegaDepth, MVS-Synth, ParallelDomain-4D, SAIL-VOS 3D, ETH3D. The PI reports approximately 1.4B image-token-equivalents were actually fed through the model during pre-training, and estimates ~4B image-token equivalents in the pre-training pool overall.

For Chinchilla-style FLOPS estimation, D ≈ 1.4 × 10^9 image-token equivalents (tokens fed).

checks.name: Customisation / Fine-tuning
checks.notes: Per the Model Readiness section, the released artifact corresponds to a single jointly-trained feed-forward model with no separate downstream fine-tunes. The project's Shaheen III GPUP objective is full fine-tuning of this base model with additional datasets (notably SA-V, Hypersim, VIPSeg, SEED4D) and new training objectives that add semantic / instance-segmentation outputs to the existing 3D reconstruction outputs.

checks.name: FLOPS Calculation
checks.notes:
Theoretical training FLOPs (6·N·D)
N = 1 × 10^9 parameters (1B, per the Model Readiness section)
D ≈ 1.4 × 10^9 image-token equivalents (tokens fed through the model during pre-training, per the Model Readiness section)

F = 6 · N · D = 6 · 1 × 10^9 · 1.4 × 10^9 = 8.4 × 10^18 FLOPs

Delivered FLOPs (GPU-hours × peak × MFU):
The applicant has requested 6 training runs × 16 nodes × 4 GPUs × 240 hours = 92,160 GH200 GPU-hours total for fine-tuning. The PI has not measured MFU yet (~95% GPU utilization reported); assuming a conservative MFU of 0.3 and GH200 BF16 peak of ~990 TFLOPS:
Delivered = 92,160 × 3,600 × 990 × 10^12 × 0.3 ≈ 9.85 × 10^22 FLOPs

Well below the 10^27 FLOPs threshold.

checks.name: Sample Inspection
checks.notes: No risk in LC 2.7 and LC 2.5 checks. Skipping inspection.

checks.name: Project & Usage Alignment
checks.notes:
Depth Anything V3 is referenced in the proposal's State-of-the-Art section [reference 5] as existing specialized geometric work that recovers spatially consistent geometry from arbitrary views using a teacher-student paradigm, used to motivate the project's goal of unifying geometry and semantics and the choice of a multi-view transformer approach (alongside VGGT and MapAnything). It is not listed in the proposal's Model Readiness additional section as a model to be deployed on Shaheen III GPUP; only MapAnything is requested.

Aligns with the approved research topic, datascience and engineering.

Country of origin: China.

D5-country affiliation found.

checks.notes:

checks.name: Training Data Documentation
checks.notes: The model is trained exclusively on public academic datasets. Pose-geometry training datasets listed by the authors include (number of scenes): AriaSyntheticENV (99,950), Objaverse (505,557), Trellis (557,408), Co3dv2 (30,616), ARKitScenes (4,388), DL3DV (6,379), WildRGBD (23,050), ScanNet++ (230), and others.

checks.name: Customisation / Fine-tuning
checks.notes: The released checkpoints are intended to work out-of-the-box without task-specific retraining. No specific fine tuning was done for the generalized variant.

checks.name: FLOPS Calculation
checks.notes:
Model parameters range from 0.35B to 1.4B across released variants.

From the paper (https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.10647), the giant variant of DA3 could take 10 days to train on 128 H100 GPUs. Considering BF16 and an MFU of 0.45:

GPU-hours = 128 GPUs × 10 days × 24 hours = 30,720 GPU-hours
GPU-seconds = 30,720 × 3,600 = 110,592,000 GPU-seconds
H100 BF16 peak = 989 TFLOPS
Effective TFLOPS (45% MFU) = 989 × 0.45 = 445 TFLOPS

Total FLOPs = 445 × 10^12 × 110,592,000 ≈ 4.92 × 10^22 FLOPs

Well below the 10^27 FLOPs threshold.

checks.name: Sample Inspection
checks.notes: No risk in LC 2.7 check. Skipping inspection.

observations:
Software: 
A docker image has been requested which can be recreated from 

The developers of VIPSeg dataset have an association with Zhejiang University (CCAI) & Baidu Research, from a D5+M country. The sample inspection revealed that the annotation of the JPG and JSON stack, which is a video dataset, was not correct. The samples checked, however, were found to be not sensitive. 
This dataset does not have a formal license attached to it. The Readme file in the GitHub repo states that it shall be only used for non-commercial use.

Depth Anything is a model maintained by Bytedance, an entity from D5+M country.
recommendation:
For VIPSeg dataset, the PI has confirmed that they will not use the annotations of the dataset. This reduces the risk of erroneous annotations which in this case become redundant and the main focus in on the visuals in the stack of JPG constituting the video dataset. 
The issue of the license is de-risked by the fact that the line in the README states the permissible terms of use to be non-commercial. 
For both the VIPSeg dataset and the Deep Anything model, the association with the D5+M country is set to low risk. The sample inspection has no sensitivity flagged, derisks the dataset. For both the dataset and the model, the PI has confirmed that there is no collaboration planned for the related entities, and there will be no sharing of the output. This de-risks both the artifacts. 

Positive check passed
Negative check passed
10^27 check passed

Recommended for the grand challenge.
